strategy: Fill gap albums: By the Numbers (0 songs) and The Talent Search (0 songs) first, then Electric Dreams, The Interview, Deep Work, The Weight, Career Moves, The Human Side.

tasks.description: Song about retention benchmarks — Lenny's most-shared content. The death tax of churn, the obsession with 'what is good,' the curves that separate winners from the dead. Read newsletters/what-is-good-retention.md for retention benchmarks across B2B/B2C/SaaS. Read newsletters/what-is-good-monthly-churn.md for churn as the silent killer of growth.

Song about activation metrics — the 'aha moment' that predicts everything. The three-step process to find the metric that tells you a user will stick. Read newsletters/how-to-determine-your-activation-metric.md for the framework. Read newsletters/what-is-a-good-activation-rate.md for activation benchmarks and common mistakes.

Song about cohort retention — the art of reading retention curves, flattening the smile, understanding what the data is really telling you. Read newsletters/how-to-measure-cohort-retention.md for the technical framework. Read newsletters/how-to-increase-your-products-retention.md for tactics to move the curves.

Song about the power of a single interview question to reveal everything about a candidate. 25 high-signal questions from 150+ podcast guests. Read newsletters/how-to-learn-the-most-about-a-candidate-from-a-single-interview-question.md for the full collection.

Song about hiring your early team — the first 10 hires that define your culture forever. Where to find them, how to convince them, when to use a recruiter. Read newsletters/hiring-your-early-team.md for the comprehensive guide. Read newsletters/winning-at-early-stage-hiring.md for the 5 themes of hiring success.

Song about the talent game from the perspective of a top executive recruiter. Finding, assessing, closing the best people. Read podcasts/lauren-ipsen.md for lessons from one of the world's top executive recruiters at Daversa Partners and General Catalyst.

Song about counterintuitive lessons from building AI products — non-determinism, thinking differently, the agency-control tradeoff. Read newsletters/counterintuitive-advice-for-building-ai-products.md for insights from 20+ AI product builders at Adobe, GitHub, Perplexity, Canva, Superhuman.

Song about Dr. Fei-Fei Li — the godmother of AI. Her journey from ImageNet to spatial intelligence, the human story behind the AI revolution. Read podcasts/dr-fei-fei-li.md for her conversation with Lenny about jobs, robots, and world models.

Song about passing first-round interviews — Lenny coached 200+ people with a 93% success rate. The employer's perspective, the MVIP framework, the art of the 45-minute window. Read newsletters/how-to-pass-any-first-round-interview-even-in-a-terrible-talent-market.md for the full guide.

Song about Job Search Councils — Phyl Terry's method of putting 6-8 job seekers together to support each other. The product lens on career transitions. Read podcasts/phyl-terry.md for negotiation tactics, job search councils, and managing emotional energy during the search.

Song about the product sense interview — the most mysterious step in the PM interview loop. Structured thinking under pressure, the art of identifying user needs in real time. Read newsletters/the-definitive-guide-to-mastering-product-sense-interviews.md for the comprehensive framework.

Song about deep work and time management — blocking time, defending it, killing distractions. Lenny's most popular post ever. Read newsletters/time-management-techniques-that-actually-work.md for the core tactics. Read newsletters/part-2-time-management-techniques-that-actually-work.md for 7 more tricks.

Song about Nir Eyal's framework — the opposite of distraction is not focus, it's traction. Intent is the one word that separates them. Read podcasts/nir-eyal.md for strategies on becoming less distracted, why to-do lists are bad, and the Indistractable framework.

Song about the Make Time philosophy from Jake Knapp and John Zeratsky — centering on one thing per day, having tactics for energy, reflecting and reframing. Read podcasts/jake-knapp--john-zeratsky.md for the Sprint/Make Time authors on making time for what matters.

Song about the hardest decisions leaders make — frameworks for deciding under uncertainty. SPADE, Coinbase framework, when to use consensus vs. authority. Read newsletters/my-favorite-decision-making-frameworks.md for battle-tested frameworks.

Song about the planning process — the W Framework from Airbnb and Eventbrite. Context, plans, integration, buy-in. The art of aligning an entire company. Read newsletters/the-secret-to-a-great-planning-process---lessons-from-airbnb-and-eventbrite.md for the full framework.

Song about influence without authority — the PM superpower. Five tactics from Slack, YouTube, and Gemini. Read newsletters/a-pms-guide-to-influence.md for the tactical guide. Read newsletters/how-to-get-better-at-influence.md for the Frodo Baggins framework.

Song about communicating tradeoffs so leaders will listen — the Ongoing Stack Rank method. Making hard choices visible, earning trust through transparency. Read newsletters/how-to-communicate-tradeoffs-so-leaders-will-listen.md for the full framework.

Song about managing nerves, anxiety, and burnout through nervous system mastery. Jonny Miller's toolkit for cultivating calm and upgrading resilience. Read podcasts/jonny-miller.md for tools on downshifting, interoception, and building capacity to hold stress.

Song about the vulnerability of asking for help at work — navigating fear, making effective asks, the irony of carrying everything alone. Read newsletters/on-asking-for-help-even-when-you-really-dont-want-to.md for the toolkit and template scripts.
